original_abstract: In this paper, we introduce BEAST 2, an open-source and modular software environment designed for Bayesian evolutionary inference. While built upon the foundation of its predecessor, BEAST 1, this new platform represents a comprehensive architectural redesign aimed at overcoming technical limitations in extensibility and long-term maintenance. A primary innovation of BEAST 2 is its integrated package management system, which empowers the research community to develop and distribute independent modules. This allows for the seamless integration of new evolutionary models and statistical methods directly into the software ecosystem without requiring core updates. We demonstrate the versatility of this framework by highlighting several newly implemented models, including advanced birth-death processes, phylodynamic tools, and multi-locus species tree inference methods. BEAST 2 provides a robust, future-proof platform for the development of sophisticated phylogenetics-based analyses.
humanized_abstract: This paper introduces BEAST 2, an open source software framework for Bayesian evolutionary analysis of trees. This application is a highly modular, fully Bayesian, Bayesian MCMC molecular phylogenetics framework, with roots in BEAST 1, but with a new, improved design that addresses the limitations of modularity and maintainability. BEAST 2 has a new, highly modular design with a package manager that allows for easy development and distribution of independent analysis modules. Models can be developed and implemented as plug-in modules that can be easily incorporated into the BEAST 2 framework, without requiring modifications to the core application. New models developed in BEAST 2 are presented, such as advanced birth-death processes, phylodynamic tools and multi-locus species tree inference methods. BEAST 2 forms a highly flexible and robust foundation for the development of many new Bayesian analysis tools for phylogenetics in the years to come.
